report_title: Samsung's One UI 8.5: A Comprehensive Analysis of New Features and Enhancements
executive_summary: Samsung's One UI 8.5 is the latest iteration of the company's mobile operating system, offering a range of new features and enhancements [1], [2], [9]. The update introduces upgraded AI features, including improved photo editing and a more refined AI experience [1], [9]. Additionally, One UI 8.5 provides a refreshed design, advanced security and privacy solutions, and improved device connectivity [1], [4], [9]. With a focus on ease of use and next-level productivity, One UI 8.5 is set to revolutionize the Samsung Galaxy experience [4], [9]. The update is expected to bring significant visual changes, including a new design language and 3D-style app icons [10], [12].

key_findings.finding_summary: One UI 8.5 introduces upgraded AI features, including improved photo editing and a more refined AI experience [1], [9]. The update also includes a new feature called Now Nudge, which provides shortcuts to the next possible action without having to jump between apps [1]. Additionally, the AI Agent takes the burden off tedious tasks, helping users get things done faster [1].

One UI 8.5 provides a refreshed design, with a focus on ease of use and next-level productivity [4], [9]. The update includes a new design language, with rounded corners, pill-shaped icons, and bottom bars [10], [12]. The Quick Panel has been redesigned, allowing users to customize it with their favorite apps [1], [9].

One UI 8.5 improves device connectivity, with new features such as Storage Share, which enables direct access to files from other Samsung devices [4], [9]. The update also includes enhancements to Quick Share, which recognizes people in photos and proactively suggests sending them directly to those contacts [4].

One UI 8.5 provides advanced security and privacy solutions, including new safeguards against theft [9]. The update also includes enhancements to privacy, with a focus on protecting user data [1], [9].

One UI 8.5 offers improved customization options, including the ability to personalize the Quick Panel, Home screen, and Lock screen [1], [9]. Users can also customize the look and feel of their device, with a range of new themes and icon packs [10], [12].

official_insights.title: One UI 8.5: A New Era of Mobile Innovation
official_insights.brief_summary: According to Samsung, One UI 8.5 is designed to provide a more intuitive and connected user experience [1]. The update includes a range of new features, including upgraded AI features, a refreshed design, and advanced security and privacy solutions [1], [2]. With a focus on ease of use and next-level productivity, One UI 8.5 is set to revolutionize the Samsung Galaxy experience [4].

official_insights.title: Samsung Introduces the New Bixby in One UI 8.5
official_insights.brief_summary: Samsung has introduced the new Bixby in One UI 8.5, which provides a more refined AI experience [2]. The update includes a range of new features, including improved voice recognition and a more intuitive interface [2].

trusted_insights.title: One UI 8.5: A Game-Changer for Samsung Galaxy Devices
trusted_insights.brief_summary: According to Android Authority, One UI 8.5 is a significant update that brings a range of new features and enhancements to Samsung Galaxy devices [9]. The update includes a new design language, improved AI features, and advanced security and privacy solutions [9]. With a focus on ease of use and next-level productivity, One UI 8.5 is set to revolutionize the Samsung Galaxy experience [9].

trusted_insights.title: One UI 8.5: A Closer Look at the New Features and Enhancements
trusted_insights.brief_summary: Android Authority has taken a closer look at the new features and enhancements in One UI 8.5, including the new design language, improved AI features, and advanced security and privacy solutions [10], [12]. The update is expected to bring significant visual changes, including a new design language and 3D-style app icons [10], [12].

cross_source_analysis: A comparison of the official and trusted sources reveals that One UI 8.5 is a significant update that brings a range of new features and enhancements to Samsung Galaxy devices [1], [2], [9]. While the official sources provide a general overview of the update, the trusted sources offer a more in-depth look at the new features and enhancements [9], [10], [12]. The update is expected to bring significant visual changes, including a new design language and 3D-style app icons [10], [12]. However, some features, such as the new Bixby, are only mentioned in the official sources [2]. Overall, the cross-source analysis suggests that One UI 8.5 is a major update that is set to revolutionize the Samsung Galaxy experience [1], [4], [9].

references.title: One UI | Samsung US
references.snippet:
One UI 8.5 comes with newly enhanced AI features, a refreshed design and advanced security and privacy solutions. Together, they help you get things done more efficiently, enjoy a more seamless and consistent experience and keep your private data better protected across your Galaxy devices.   
    
   • AI features: introduces upgraded Photo Assist for smarter photo editing, along with other features like Now Nudge for a more refined AI experience. Explore more AI features that make your life smarter and easier in One UI 8.5.   
    
   Go to Galaxy AI section   
    
   • Design: provides a wider view of the screen and a smoother user experience with redesigned navigation bars and menu.   
    
   Go to UI section [...] One UI is Samsung's innovative software, designed for intuitive and connected user experience on your Galaxy devices. With Galaxy AI deeply integrated, One UI 8.5 helps you get through your day quicker and more effortlessly by providing instant assistance.   
    
   • The upgraded One UI 8.5 now understands your usage patterns and your needs, delivering a more personalized and convenient experience.   
    
   • With advanced AI features like Now Nudge, you can easily access shortcuts to the next possible action without having to jump between apps.   
    
   • AI Agent takes the burden off your tedious tasks, helping you get things done faster. Simply tap or enter a short prompt to get what you need. [...] With One UI 8.5, you can make your device truly yours by personalizing the Quick Panel, Home screen and Lock screen. Enjoy easier access to everyday controls with a more balanced look.   
    
   • Quick Panel: Customize the Quick Panel by adding or deleting items to get a quick access to your favorite apps. You can also resize and rearrange the brightness and sound bars to optimize your viewing experience.   
    
   • Home screen & Lock screen: When you set the wallpaper, the image is automatically repositioned to align better with key apps and widgets on your screen, delivering a cleaner layout."

references.title: Samsung Begins Official Rollout of One UI 8 to Galaxy Devices
references.snippet:
### UX Optimized for Multiple Form Factors

One UI 8 redefines versatility with UX optimized across the Galaxy product portfolio. Designed to adapt to the unique form factors of Galaxy smartphones and tablets, One UI 8 ensures seamless functionality while boosting productivity and efficiency. [...] Samsung Electronics today announced the official rollout of One UI 8 — introducing advanced multimodal AI capabilities, a UX optimized for different device form factors and personalized, proactive suggestions to make everyday life smarter and more convenient. Starting this week with the Galaxy S25 series, One UI 8 will also reach the Galaxy S24 series, Galaxy Z Fold6, Galaxy Z Flip6, Galaxy S24 FE and additional eligible models later this year.1

### Personalized Suggestions With Advanced Security [...] ### Intelligent Multimodality: AI That Works the Way the User Does

Powered by multimodal AI designed to deliver experiences tailored to user needs, One UI 8 transforms the way users interact with Galaxy devices. By combining visual, auditory and contextual intelligence, it makes everyday tasks intuitive and effortless.

# Samsung Launches One UI 8.5 Beta for Next-Level Ease of Use

Latest version of One UI brings enhanced productivity, privacy, and performance across the Galaxy ecosystem

12/8/2025 [...] Sharing is smoother too, thanks to enhancements to Quick Share2 which now recognizes people in photos and proactively suggests sending them directly to those contacts.

Galaxy AI 

##### How does One UI 8.5 improve device connectivity?

With Storage share new cross-device features make managing devices easier, whether users need to manage files, share their network, or communicate with nearby devices. Audio Broadcast3 enables effortless communication with LE Audio-supported devices nearby using Auracast. In addition to audio from media sources, users can now broadcast their voice using their Galaxy smartphone’s built-in microphone — ideal for group situations like tours or events.

references.title: Breaking: Samsung kicks off One UI 8.5 beta program for Galaxy S25 series - Android Authority
references.snippet:
New Galaxy AI features, including improved image generation tools.
 Better cross-device connectivity, like storage sharing between Samsung devices and faster Smart View access.
 Enhanced Auracast features that make it easier to listen to and broadcast sound through the new Audio broadcast menu in Settings.
 Battery and performance upgrades, including redesigned power management controls.
 Enhanced customization, with more control over the Quick panel, lock screen, widgets, and icons.
 Enhancements to privacy and security, including new safeguards against theft.
 Accessibility improvements, such as wider support for hearing devices and new control options. [...] You can also expect a significant visual redesign in One UI 8.5, including transparency elements and refreshed 3D-style app icons and UI components. Check out the full official One UI 8.5 beta changelog in the screenshots we’ve captured below:

In its announcement post, Samsung highlights the updated Photo Assist feature, which allows users to continuously generate new images without needing to save each iteration, as they can select their favorites later on. Samsung is also highlighting the Storage Share feature, which enables direct access to files from other Samsung devices, including TVs, within the My Files app. [...] Today’s One UI 8.5 beta program rollout comes just days after a major changelog leak revealed all the new and upcoming features of the update. We can now confirm that the leaked changelog was mostly accurate. It also mentioned some Bixby improvements, but we’re not seeing them in the official changelog pasted below.

The software introduces a substantial visual refresh, new AI-powered tools, deeper connectivity capabilities, and improved customization options. Since the update is based on Android 16 QPR2, it’s also expected to bring major new Android features to Galaxy devices.

## What to expect in One UI 8.5

Samsung is bringing the following new features with the latest One UI 8.5 update:

references.title: New Samsung One UI 8.5 leak hints at a bigger redesign than we first thought - Android Authority
references.snippet:
> Great News!
>
> One UI 8.5: Calculator app gets an update to v12.5.10.18.
>
> What’s new:  
>  • Refreshed UI  
>  • New UI effects  
>  • New 3D app icon
>
> Repost pic.twitter.com/OZ1X97m6bK
>
> — Tarun Vats (@tarunvats33) December 4, 2025

The selected button in the Calculator app is also highlighted in a bluish-green gradient, while the app now has a new fluid animation to accommodate the numbers or text being selected. In addition, the Calculator’s icon also appears to have undergone a refresh and now looks more volumetric and three-dimensional. However, these changes are much less prominent when viewed with a dark theme.

Here’s a detailed comparison between different screens. [...] Besides being painted in more colors, the timer and the stopwatch sections of the Clock app now show more detailed graphics with accents around the clock dial. Another slight change comes in the form of the boxes now allocated to the different time zones in the world clock, which previously only appeared as text.

In addition to these changes, Samsung might also bring a bigger collection of backgrounds for the Samsung Internet app, as Ice Universe highlights in a separate Weibo post. Meanwhile, it is also expected to undergo some visual upgrades, including the repositioning of the address bar to somewhere near the center of the page, and also adding a shadow underneath it for similar 3D effects.

SamsungSamsung One UI [...] Here’s a detailed comparison between different screens.

Apart from the visual changes, the Calculator app in One UI 8.5 will also access the phone’s clipboard, allowing it to suggest numbers or formulas from copied text.

Simultaneously, Samsung is bringing similar changes to the Clock app, as highlighted by leaker Ice Universe on X. Here, the 3D-fication is less prominent compared to the Calculator. Instead of relying on more shadows, Samsung appears to be using more vibrant colors across the board compared to the existing One UI 8 interface.

# Samsung's One UI 8.5 update makes quick settings pop with cleaner, glassier design

But not as iOS-esque as some of the other One UI 8.5 features.

By

•

12 hours ago

•

•

Sammobile

TL;DR

 Samsung appears to have further reworked the Quick Settings panel in the latest One UI 8.5 build.
 The leak shows dark outlines outside Quick Settings tiles along with a subtle, glass-like interface.
 This adds to the list of features seemingly inspired by Apple’s latest design changes in iOS 26, though it’s not entirely the same. [...] While a revamped Quick Settings panel has already been spotted in previously leaked builds of One UI 8.5, the latest leak gives us a better look at additional options it could bring. Tipster Tarun Vats shared a screenshot of the updated panel on X, showing that quick settings tiles for various shortcuts now have a thin outline. This is predominantly visible when the shortcut is disabled.

While it’s a subtle touch, the outline also visually separates the tile from the background. Additionally, it should be slightly easier on the eyes, especially when you’re haphazardly searching for the right one.

references.snippet:
Samsung’s next major software update — One UI 8.5 — is starting to take shape, and we can now see how it’s coming together thanks to a new hands-on video posted by folks over at Sammobile. While we’ve also leaked several upcoming One UI 8.5 features exclusively, like the new Privacy Display, NFC-based Quick Share, Automatic Call Screening, and more, and this hands-on doesn’t show any of them, it gives us our clearest look yet at some big UI changes Galaxy users can expect from the update when it arrives.

TL;DR

 A leaked One UI 8.5 build on the Galaxy S25 Ultra shows Samsung’s big design refresh, which includes rounded corners, pill-shaped icons, and bottom bars.
 Sammobile has showcased the update in a hands-on video, giving us a better look at features like the fully customizable quick settings panel, Direct Voicemail in the Phone app, and new tweaks in Gallery, My Files, and Camera.
 More features are expected before launch, with a stable rollout most likely coming alongside the Galaxy S26 series. [...] While this early build doesn’t represent the final software, and as we said, it doesn’t showcase a number of new features we’re expecting to see in One UI 8.5, it gives us a good preview of where the software is headed. Samsung seems to be unifying the look and feel of its apps with a cleaner, more modern visual language, reminiscent of Google’s Material 3 Expressive refresh and even some iOS 26 influences. We expect to see more features added as the update develops.

Earlier this month, Samsung released the fourth beta of One UI 8.5 for the Galaxy S25 family, and today the fifth beta is out to those who enrolled their devices in the beta program. The new firmware ends in ZZAN and includes the February 5, 2026 security patch level.

It also comes with an updated version of Bixby. The rollout is currently taking place in the US, UK, India, Germany, and Korea. The new build is about 546-553MB in size. [...] Total reader comments: 3

references.title: Samsung's One UI 8.5 update is an even bigger deal than you thought - Android Authority
references.snippet:
We’re still over 3 months away from the expected unveiling of the Samsung Galaxy S26 series, but we already know many of its new software tricks thanks to a trove of One UI 8.5 leaks. Leaked builds of the software have surfaced, giving us a rare opportunity to see what Samsung is developing for its next major release.

As its name suggests, One UI 8.5 will be based on this year’s Android 16 release, not the yet-to-be-released Android 17. However, it will differ from its predecessor. While One UI 8 was based on the initial stable release of Android 16, One UI 8.5 will be built upon the second quarterly platform release, Android 16 QPR2. [...] That’s why Samsung basing its One UI 8.5 release on Android 16 QPR2 is such a big deal. We haven’t seen the company — or any other OEM for that matter — incorporate changes from a quarterly Android release since the limited Android 12L release in early 2022. And considering Samsung has embraced Google’s Trunk Stable model to speed up software updates, this may be just the beginning. Moving forward, Samsung could continue to match Google’s release cadence, allowing users and developers to get new Android features on Galaxy devices much sooner.

Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority? [...] •

•

Mishaal Rahman / Android Authority

TL;DR

 Leaked builds show Samsung’s next One UI 8.5 update, debuting with the Galaxy S26, will be based on Android 16’s second quarterly release (QPR2).
 This is significant because most device makers skip quarterly Android updates, delaying new features until the next major annual version is released.
 By adopting QPR2, Samsung can bring new Android platform features to Galaxy devices much faster than before, matching Google’s own release cadence.

references.title: The One UI 8.5 beta turned my Samsung phone into an iPhone - Android Authority
references.snippet:
However, it’s also true that Samsung going to such lengths to copy iOS 26 is a bad look. Samsung is far from the only Android OEM to tweak its software this year to be more iOS-like, but it stings a little more given Samsung’s size and scale, plus its abandonment of the original design elements that still existed in One UI.

One UI had a clear-cut design identity when it debuted in 2018, and while it stayed that way for a while, Samsung has chipped away at it here and there with updates over the years. One UI 8.5 feels like the largest dose of iOS-ification we’ve seen from Samsung at once, and it’s clear Samsung doesn’t intend to change this strategy anytime soon. [...] That feeling is even more prevalent in some of Samsung’s first-party applications— specifically the Clock, Gallery, and Phone apps. All of these are redesigned in One UI 8.5, with a new bottom navigation bar that looks like it was ripped straight from iOS 26. The floating bar design is functionally sound and doesn’t really change how you use any of these apps, but that almost makes it worse.

Samsung may not admit it, but it’s pretty evident that the only reason these apps have this new navigation bar is that the iPhone does. And it would be naive to think that more Samsung apps won’t adopt this new UI in the weeks/months ahead. [...] And the iOS 26 inspiration doesn’t end there. The search bar in the app drawer is now a smaller pill shape, akin to the iOS 26-like navigation bar in the apps mentioned above. The back arrow in the Settings app is now a floating circle, just like in iOS 26. Samsung even went so far as to add a glow effect to the Calculator app’s buttons, mimicking the Liquid Glass design of the iOS 26 Calculator app.

## Samsung is losing its software design identity

Do these iOS inspirations make One UI 8.5 a bad piece of software? Do they ruin what’s an otherwise solid update? No, and I don’t think anyone would suggest that.

One UI 8.5 is already one of my favorite versions of Samsung’s Android skin, and the updated Good Lock modules are only making me love it even more. What about you? Are you looking forward to trying out the new features from One UI 8.5 and Good Lock? Let us know in the comments.

Good LockSamsungSamsung One UI

Thank you for being part of our community. Read our Comment Policy before posting. [...] Hadlee Simons / Android Authority

When Samsung releases new versions of One UI, it usually breaks some of the Good Lock modules I use every day. The One UI 8.5 beta is no exception — several Good Lock modules have been incompatible since the beta started, including Home Up. Home Up is a module that lets you tweak everything from your home screen icons to the overview menu. Samsung recently updated Home Up, and it does more than make it compatible with the latest version of One UI — it adds new features that have made my favorite Good Lock module better than ever.
